title: Asuka and Rei in Anime Map
display_title: Asuka and Rei in Anime Map
wikitext:
[]: Asuka and Rei in Anime Map             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Jun 25, 2011 2:52 pm http://www.jnto.go.jp/eng/animemap/index.html Reportage by DarkMirage http://www.darkmirage.com/2011/06/20/japan-anime-map I downloaded the map after I saw the girls.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Jun 25, 2011 10:29 pm Anime tourism is a great idea. Glad that Japan National Tourism Organization , a government body, is getting acts behind it !! Those are the "anime pilgrimage sites" and of course we can see the most famous ones like Haruhi and Lucky Stars there. But where is the most famous anime known for the pilgrimage zeal of its fans -- K-On ???

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 25, 2011 10:30 pm This is what the map proper is about, hope it helps (one of the posts near the end have a link to a website that discusses each location/Hakone Pilgrimage): http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7187

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sun Jun 26, 2011 5:39 am Anime tourism map...?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sun Jun 26, 2011 11:05 am Someone wrote: Anime tourism map...?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sun Jun 26, 2011 11:08 am soul.assassin wrote: That's right. Profit. :) It has become something of a tradition since Lucky Star (and maybe Field of Dreams decades before), in which fans would, basing on the locales depicted in their favorite show, make an actual pilgrimage around the location, taking pictures of the exact spot and frame where a certain scene was shown.

Those are the "anime pilgrimage sites" and of course we can see the most famous ones like Haruhi and Lucky Stars there. But where is the most famous anime known for the pilgrimage zeal of its fans -- K-On ???

Profit. :) It has become something of a tradition since Lucky Star (and maybe Field of Dreams decades before), in which fans would, basing on the locales depicted in their favorite show, make an actual pilgrimage around the location, taking pictures of the exact spot and frame where a certain scene was shown.
